answerBox: University Circle is an East Side Cleveland neighborhood anchored by Case Western Reserve University, the Cleveland Museum of Art, and the Cleveland Clinic, served by the RTA Red Line and HealthLine. Citywide, a 3-bedroom house rents for a median of $1,350 a month per Zumper's July 2026 report; we manage voucher-friendly 3-bedroom homes in nearby Hough and Glenville.

body: University Circle is one of Cleveland's East Side neighborhoods, built around a cluster of major institutions: **Case Western Reserve University**, the **Cleveland Museum of Art**, the **Severance Music Center**, and the Cleveland Clinic's main campus sits just to the south. The area itself is dominated by these institutions rather than large stretches of rental housing stock.

We don't currently manage listings inside University Circle itself, but we do manage voucher-friendly 3-bedroom rental homes in the directly adjoining East Side neighborhoods of **Hough** and **Glenville** — both within a short distance of University Circle's institutions. If a 3-bedroom house near this area is what you need, [tell our team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll match you with current availability nearby.

sections.h2: How much does a 3-bedroom house rent for near here?
body: Citywide, Zumper's rent report dated July 4, 2026 puts Cleveland's median 3-bedroom asking rent at **$1,350 a month**. RentCafe (Yardi), reporting July 2, 2026, shows a much higher $2,716 average for 3-bedroom units — but that figure reflects larger professionally managed apartment buildings near University Circle's institutional core, not the smaller houses found in the surrounding residential streets.

In nearby Hough and Glenville, where we do manage rental homes, currently available 3-bedroom houses across our Cleveland portfolio generally run **$1,100 to $1,500 a month**, tracking closer to the Zumper citywide median than the large-apartment RentCafe figure.

rows: Citywide median asking rent, all housing types
Currently available 3-bedroom rental homes, incl. nearby Hough/Glenville

body: University Circle is one of the best-connected parts of Cleveland for transit. The RTA **Red Line** (heavy rail) runs through the area on its route between Cleveland Hopkins Airport, Downtown's Tower City, and Windermere in East Cleveland. The **HealthLine**, a bus rapid transit route running in a dedicated center median along Euclid Avenue, connects Public Square directly to University Circle and on to East Cleveland — making it possible to reach the area's institutions without a car from many parts of the East Side.

items: **Key institutions:** Case Western Reserve University, Cleveland Museum of Art, Severance Music Center
**Bus rapid transit:** HealthLine along Euclid Avenue
**Adjacent neighborhoods with rentals we manage:** Hough and Glenville

body: Every rental home we manage in Hough and Glenville — the neighborhoods bordering University Circle — accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, and Cleveland has not adopted a citywide ordinance requiring landlords to accept vouchers, so it's worth confirming a specific landlord's policy if you're looking outside what we manage. See our [Hough houses for rent](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/), [Glenville houses for rent](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-glenville-cleveland/), or our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for more detail.

sections.h2: Finding a 3-bedroom house near University Circle
body: Since we don't have listings directly inside University Circle, the best next step is to reach out with your bedroom count, budget, and timeline so we can point you to current availability in Hough, Glenville, or elsewhere on Cleveland's East Side. You can also browse [houses for rent across Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) to compare neighborhoods.

faq.q: Are there 3-bedroom houses for rent inside University Circle?
faq.a: University Circle itself is largely made up of institutional campuses — Case Western Reserve University, the Cleveland Museum of Art, and the Cleveland Clinic — with limited standalone rental housing. We manage voucher-friendly 3-bedroom houses in the adjacent Hough and Glenville neighborhoods, both a short distance away.

faq.q: How much does a 3-bedroom house cost near University Circle?
faq.a: Citywide, Cleveland's median 3-bedroom asking rent was $1,350 a month per Zumper's July 2026 report. Currently available 3-bedroom homes in our managed portfolio, including nearby Hough and Glenville, generally run $1,100 to $1,500 a month.

faq.q: How do I get to University Circle without a car?
faq.a: The RTA Red Line (heavy rail) and the HealthLine bus rapid transit route on Euclid Avenue both serve University Circle, connecting it directly to Downtown's Tower City and other parts of the East Side.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage homes near University Circle?
faq.a: We manage voucher-friendly rental homes in Hough and Glenville, both neighborhoods bordering University Circle, rather than inside the institutional core itself. Tell us your bedroom needs and budget and we can point you to current availability nearby.

faq.q: Are homes near University Circle Section 8 friendly?
faq.a: Every home we manage in nearby Hough and Glenville accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, so policies can vary for properties outside what we manage.
